question_text: A student compares two scenarios: Scenario 1 has oxygen gas at 25°C in a 2-litre container at 101 kPa. Scenario 2 has the same oxygen gas at 75°C in a 1-litre container. Using the particle model and kinetic theory, which scenario has the higher pressure? Show your reasoning by considering both the effect of temperature on particle speed and the effect of volume on particle density.

options.label: A
options.text: Scenario 1, because lower temperature means particles move slower and create less pressure

options.label: B
options.text: Scenario 2, because higher temperature increases particle speed and smaller volume increases particle density, both increasing pressure

options.label: C
options.text: Both scenarios have equal pressure because the temperature increase cancels out the volume decrease

options.label: D
options.text: Scenario 1, because a larger volume means particles are more spread out and collide more frequently with the walls

correct_answer: B) Scenario 2, because higher temperature increases particle speed and smaller volume increases particle density, both increasing pressure
explanation: Scenario 2 has two factors that increase pressure: heating particles from 25°C to 75°C makes them move faster (more forceful collisions), and halving the volume from 2 litres to 1 litre doubles the particle density (more frequent collisions). Both effects work together to significantly increase pressure in Scenario 2.
